meaning_english: Well-meaning and kindly. Antonyms: Malevolent, Miserly, Cruel, Selfish.
Having an exaggerated sense of one's own importance. Antonyms: Humble, Modest, Meek, Diffident.
Careful to avoid potential problems or dangers. Antonyms: Reckless, Rash, Impetuous, Heedless.
A very large quantity of something. Antonyms: Scarcity, Dearth, Paucity, Shortage.
A person who tends to be hopeful and confident. Antonyms: Pessimist, Cynic, Doomsayer, Gloomy.
Capable of bending easily without breaking. Antonyms: Rigid, Stiff, Adamant, Inflexible.
Allowing light to pass through so that objects behind can be distinctly seen. Antonyms: Opaque, Cloudy, Obscure, Hazy.
Made or produced by human beings rather than occurring naturally. Antonyms: Natural, Genuine, Authentic, Real.
Enough or more than enough; plentiful. Antonyms: Meagre, Scant, Insufficient, Sparse.
Far away in space or time. Antonyms: Adjacent, Near, Close, Proximate.
Of very great size or substance; huge. Antonyms: Tiny, Minute, Dwarf, Puny.
The state of being in agreement or concord. Antonyms: Discord, Conflict, Strife, Friction.
An act of defeating an enemy or opponent. Antonyms: Defeat, Failure, Loss, Debacle.
Behavior showing high moral standards. Antonyms: Vice, Wickedness, Immorality, Sin.
Uncertain, indefinite, or unclear character or meaning. Antonyms: Clear, Precise, Definite, Explicit.
Not likely to change or fail; firmly established. Antonyms: Unsteady, Volatile, Shaky, Precarious.
Intense, strict, or harsh. Antonyms: Mild, Gentle, Lenient, Moderate.
Having an uneven or irregular surface. Antonyms: Smooth, Sleek, Polished, Level.
Happening in a short time or at a great speed. Antonyms: Sluggish, Slow, Tardy, Leisurely.
The state of being united or joined as a whole. Antonyms: Division, Separation, Discord, Fragmentation.
Risky; dangerous. Antonyms: Safe, Secure, Harmless, Protected.
Not discovered or known about; uncertain. Antonyms: Famous, Notorious, Apparent, Obvious.
Required by law or rules; compulsory. Antonyms: Optional, Voluntary, Discretionary, Arbitrary.
Showing or feeling opposition or dislike; unfriendly. Antonyms: Amiable, Friendly, Cordial, Gentle.
Easily broken or damaged. Antonyms: Sturdy, Robust, Tough, Durable.
Sparing or economical as regards money or food. Antonyms: Extravagant, Lavish, Wasteful, Prodigal.
Very large in size, quantity, or extent. Antonyms: Tiny, Minute, Small, Negligible.
Characterized by constant change, activity, or progress. Antonyms: Static, Dormant, Fixed, Inert.
The complete loss or absence of hope. Antonyms: Hope, Optimism, Cheer, Confidence.
The ability to do something that frightens one. Antonyms: Cowardice, Fear, Timidity, Meekness.
A mass departure of people. Antonyms: Arrival, Influx, Entry, Admission.
Affected by lethargy; sluggish and apathetic. Antonyms: Energetic, Vivacious, Active, Spirited.
The lowest point in the fortunes of a person or organization. Antonyms: Zenith, Peak, Summit, Acme.
Not easily upset or excited. Antonyms: Turbulent, Stormy, Agitated, Violent.
Keeping careful watch for possible danger or difficulties. Antonyms: Negligent, Careless, Heedless, Lax.
